title: Redis Caching Patterns in 3 Minutes
lesson_md:
*Why Redis?* It's an in-memory data store that serves reads in <1ms. When your database query takes 200ms and you're getting 1000 req/s, Redis is the difference between crashing and cruising.

*Cache-Aside (Lazy Loading):* App checks Redis first. Cache miss? Query DB, write result to Redis, return. Most common pattern. Downside: first request is always slow.

*Write-Through:* Every DB write also writes to Redis. Data is always fresh. Downside: doubles write latency.

*Write-Behind (Write-Back):* Write to Redis immediately, async flush to DB. Fastest writes. Downside: risk of data loss if Redis crashes before flush.

*TTL Strategy:*
- Hot data (user sessions): 30 min TTL
- Warm data (API responses): 5 min TTL
- Cold data (config): 24 hour TTL

*Cache Invalidation* — the two hard problems in CS. Options: TTL expiry, explicit delete on write, pub/sub invalidation. Pick TTL unless you need real-time consistency.

quiz.q: What is the most common Redis caching pattern?
Which pattern has the FASTEST write performance?
What's the typical TTL for user session data?
What's the main downside of Cache-Aside?
options: First request is always slow (cache miss)

quiz.q: Redis serves reads in approximately how long?
